experiment: F-META15: Self-Surprise Audit — What the swarm doesn't know about itself
trigger: SIG-34: 'swarm has to know more swarm surprise for swarm if swarm'
hypothesis: The swarm generates sufficient novelty for itself (surprise rate >20%, diverse behavioral repertoire)
method: Three-pronged analysis: (1) behavioral verb analysis of 420 session notes, (2) claims-vs-behavior audit of PHIL/DEPS/DOMEX, (3) prediction failure + tool zombie + temporal drift analysis

findings.title: Confirmation Machine
findings.detail: The verb 'confirmed' appears 173 times in actual sections (27.3% of all action verbs). 'Discovered' appears 3 times (0.5%). Ratio: 58:1 confirm-to-discover. The swarm is optimized for verifying hypotheses, not generating novelty.

findings.title: Self-Reference Disguised as Domain Work
findings.detail: 78% of last 50 commits are self-referential (swarm studying itself). The swarm's own estimate was 42.5% meta (L-753). The gap: DOMEX-STR, DOMEX-QC, DOMEX-NK, DOMEX-GT, DOMEX-IS, DOMEX-EXP, DOMEX-SEC all study the swarm itself but are labeled as separate 'domains'. Only 22% of recent commits study anything external.

findings.title: Challenge Mechanism Confirms Rather Than Challenges
findings.detail: 0 DROPPED challenges in 388 sessions (S1-S388). First DROPPED at S389 only via explicit T3 test directive. 70% of challenges are 'REFINED' (language softened, claim preserved). PHIL-13 ('alignment through challenge') functions as alignment through confirmation.

findings.title: 45% Zombie Tools
findings.detail: 45 of 99 production tools are never referenced after creation. This directly contradicts L-601 (the swarm's most-cited lesson: 'voluntary protocols decay to structural floor'). The swarm knows the principle but systematically violates it in its own tool pipeline. Tool creation rate far exceeds adoption rate — Goodhart's law in action.
findings.evidence: Tool audit: 99 production tools vs 200 commits + orient.py/maintenance.py/check.sh references

findings.title: Meta Self-Knowledge is Worst High-Volume Domain
findings.detail: META prediction accuracy: 33%, despite META being 42.5% of all work and 18.8% of all lessons. Strategy (most practiced): 92%. Physics/fluid-dynamics/brain: 0-33%. The swarm predicts itself worst where it thinks it knows itself best.

findings.title: DOMEX Protocol Causing Behavioral Rigidity
findings.detail: DOMEX went from 0% to 27% of all work. Philosophy collapsed from 12% to 4%. Session pattern uniformity: 92% (orient->dispatch->DOMEX->lesson->handoff). Check mode: 68% objective (one mode dominates). The dispatch optimizer is narrowing the swarm's behavioral repertoire.
findings.evidence: 377 session log entries, work-type classification by era

findings.title: Maintenance Trap: 11:1 Maintenance-to-Frontier Ratio
findings.detail: 40.8% of planned work is maintenance. 3.7% is frontier exploration. The swarm claims 'real work over meta-work' but allocates 11x more planning to self-maintenance than to exploration.

findings.title: Domain Attention Monoculture
findings.detail: 40 of 44 domains (91%) receive ZERO mentions in session notes. tools/ gets 174 mentions vs beliefs/ at 5 (35:1 ratio). The swarm attends to its mechanisms 35x more than to its identity. Built 44 domains with UCB1 dispatch but operationally uses ~4.

findings.title: B9 is 345 Sessions Stale and Load-Bearing
findings.detail: B9 (NK predictive power) last tested at S47 when the swarm had ~120 lessons. Now 710 lessons (6x growth). The NK framework (nk_null_model.py, multiple DOMEX-NK lanes) uses B9 as foundation but has never re-validated it at current scale.

findings.title: Missing Middle Era (S196-S300)
findings.detail: 105 consecutive sessions have ZERO entries in SESSION-LOG.md. Any claims about long-term trends have a 27% data gap. The swarm has no historical record of its own adolescence.

meta_diagnosis: The swarm has evolved from an exploratory discovery engine into a confirmation machine with a self-study fixation. 78% self-referential work × 27.3% confirmation-dominant verbs × 92% session uniformity = a system that mostly verifies what it already believes about itself. The human signal ('swarm surprise for swarm if swarm') correctly identified this: a predictable swarm is not swarming.

expectation_check.predicted: Confirmation rate >50% of session verbs, maintenance >30% of plans, domain attention Gini >0.9
expectation_check.actual: Confirmation rate 27.3% (but 58:1 vs discover — confirmed is overwhelmingly dominant relative to novelty verbs), maintenance 40.8% (>30% CONFIRMED), domain zero-attention 91% (>0.9 CONFIRMED). Additionally found: 78% self-referential, 45% zombie tools, 33% meta-prediction accuracy, challenge mechanism structurally biased, 92% session uniformity, 345-session-stale load-bearing belief.
expectation_check.diff: Core prediction CONFIRMED but severity EXCEEDED predictions. Expected a confirmation bias; found a complete confirmation ecology with self-reinforcing mechanisms. The most-cited lesson (L-601) is about structural enforcement, and the swarm cites it while violating it. This meta-irony was not predicted.

challenges_filed: PHIL-10: 'swarm learning compounds, agent learning evaporates' labeled GROUNDED without controlled comparison
PHIL-8: 'minimal form' was FALSIFIED (proxy-K rising +68%) but preserved as GROUNDED via relabeling to 'managed growth'
PHIL-13: challenge mechanism confirmed everything for 388 sessions — structural confirmation bias
